era_context: Identical to -2445: Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI), Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin, mature Harappan civilization, and the traditional period of the Xia Dynasty in China. This year falls within the same broad historical moment across multiple civilizations.

events.title: Pepi II of Egypt — continued reign
events.description: Pepi II continues his reign as pharaoh of Egypt. No specific events are documented for this particular year in surviving sources. His reign is characterized by the maintenance of Old Kingdom administrative structures and the construction of smaller pyramids compared to earlier dynasties, reflecting a gradual decline in centralized power and resources.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for 2446 BCE. The year is assigned based on the traditional chronology of Pepi II's reign, but with the same ±30–50 year uncertainty as -2445.

events.title: Akkadian Empire — military campaigns and consolidation
events.description: Naram-Sin continues his military campaigns to consolidate Akkadian control. During his reign, he conducted campaigns against the Lullubi (in the Zagros Mountains), the Guti, and various city-states in the Levant. These campaigns are documented in royal inscriptions and the famous Naram-Sin Stele, which depicts him as a deified ruler. The exact chronology of individual campaigns is uncertain, but they occurred throughout his reign (c. 2254–2218 BCE).
events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's military campaigns are well-attested, but the specific year-by-year chronology is not preserved. Assignment to 2446 BCE is conventional.

events.title: Harappan trade networks — continued operation
events.description: The Harappan civilization maintains extensive trade networks with Mesopotamia, the Persian Gulf, and Central Asia. Archaeological evidence shows the exchange of goods including lapis lazuli from Afghanistan, carnelian from the Deccan, and Harappan seals and pottery in Mesopotamian sites. This trade is facilitated by maritime routes and overland caravans, indicating sophisticated commercial organization.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence of Harappan seals and pottery in Mesopotamian sites (Ur, Uruk, Lagash)
Mesopotamian texts mentioning trade with Meluhha

events.certainty_note: Trade networks are well-established through archaeological evidence. The specific year 2446 BCE is assigned by convention within the mature Harappan phase.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence for events in 2446 BCE, as no particular events are documented for this year. The same chronological uncertainties apply as for -2445.
historiographic_note: This year is even more poorly documented than -2445, as no specific events are recorded. The historical record for this period is fragmentary and relies on the same sources as the previous year. The assignment of events to this year is largely conventional and reflects the broader era rather than year-specific developments.

graph_edges.note: Pepi II's reign continues without documented change
